escalation.rationale: US-Iran two-week ceasefire agreement brokered by Pakistan temporarily reduces tensions despite ongoing strikes in Lebanon and Ukraine.

ticker_items: US-Iran agree two-week ceasefire, Strait of Hormuz reopens
Israeli strikes kill 254 in Lebanon, national mourning declared
Russia launches spring offensive with 400 drones on Ukraine
Pakistan emerges as key mediator in US-Iran truce talks

new_entrant.text: Yemen's Houthis officially joined Iran in coordinated strikes against Israel alongside Hezbollah.

eastern_europe.description: Russian attacks on southeastern Ukraine killed eight people and injured over two dozen.

middle_east.description: Israeli attacks across Lebanon killed at least 254 people in single day operations.

claimed_totals.figure: 2,076 Iranian dead, 26 Israeli, 13 US soldiers, 28 Gulf states

observers.description: Pakistan successfully brokered the US-Iran two-week ceasefire agreement. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif confirmed Pakistan will host negotiations to end the Middle East war.
China's Iran peace initiative failed to gain headlines despite diplomatic efforts. Beijing continues abstaining on key UN Security Council resolutions regarding the conflict.

financial.notes: Strait of Hormuz reopening provides immediate relief to exporters and reduces shipping disruption costs.

update_log.text: Lebanon declares national mourning after Israeli strikes kill 254 people in single day.
Gulf states express wariness over US-Iran ceasefire giving Iran leverage over Hormuz strait.
Pakistan confirms role as key broker in securing US-Iran two-week truce agreement.
